achievements.description: GDCU-8931 Strategy pages do not appear in search — Done at 09:32 (Harden Thomas Wallace). Strategy hub search visibility fix complete across all pages.
GDCU-8900 SlotsLaunch Game Grid Shortcode — Done at 11:07 (Kelvin Yee). Hot-flagged ticket; SlotsLaunch game grid integration complete.
GDCU-8879 Create Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card — Done at 11:40 (Jessica Dordevic). UX card for brand carousel complete, unblocking GDCU-8861 dev implementation.
GDCU-8865 Implement Game/Software Provider Names and Improve Sizing for Slot Games Hub — Done at 11:50 (Harden Thomas Wallace). Provider names and sizing improvements live for GX.
GDCU-8733 Apply Feedback to the Learning Hub — Done at 13:59 (Carlos Blauth). Cascade win: unblocked by GDCU-8736 being approved earlier today.

GDCU-8736 Share/Create Imagery for the Learning Hub — Done at 14:45 (Ciara Carroll). After a live review session via group DM (Mike, Ciara, Michael Butlin) in which Michael Butlin gave feedback on imagery (blackjack wheel, Myth Buster purple colouring), Ciara iterated and Michael gave final sign-off at ~14:43. Cascade: GDCU-8733 also closed.
GDCU-8877 Add Field to Manage Phone Numbers on Email Confirmation Pages in Admin Panel — Done at 15:37 (Novica Vukobratovic). Was Day 2 in Mike's In Product review queue; cleared today.
GDCU-8849 Add New Style to Author Carousel Shortcode — Done at 16:08 (Harden Thomas Wallace). All-geos author carousel styling complete.
GDCU-8861 Integrate Brand Carousel Component — Done at 16:25 (Harden Thomas Wallace). Hot-flagged. All-geos brand carousel dev implementation complete.

GDCU-8834 Implement Fixes for Cool Tones Update — Done at 16:53 (Harden Thomas Wallace). Partial release confirmed for tomorrow; remaining comment-section UX fixes deferred to next week pending Jessica's feedback.
GDCU-8929 Enable Sidebar Navigation to Casino and Betting Strategy Articles — Done at 16:53 (Harden Thomas Wallace). GX sidebar nav to strategy articles complete.

IUJ Banners (GDCU-8928) — decision resolved after 7 days outstanding. Mike and Jessica aligned in group DM at ~12:01-12:50: 'Get My Free Chips' CTA kept only on the non-profile-page banner; other banner links to profile home page as-is. Jessica: 'I prepared it for dev and will update the ticket.' Ticket moved to In Product at 14:43.

GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign — Day 21 blocker PARTIALLY RESOLVED. Mike nudged Ciara and Michael Butlin in group DM at 16:04. Ciara responded: 'i have the solution, we discussed it Mike. just needs some updates so i'll sort that now.' Ticket moved from Blocked → In Progress at 16:06.

Standard.co.uk (Indy group) new partnership announced by Gabriel Cornoiu in today's Mid Week Catchup. Agreement in place; integration/setup to kick off soon. Product and design team involvement confirmed needed; Eoin McCarthy to create setup tickets for next sprint.

14-ticket release confirmed for tomorrow Thu 07/05 by Chloe Christie in the Mid Week Catchup and #6amigos. BAU: GDCU-8895, GDCU-7363, GDCU-8611, GDCU-8854, GDCU-8912, GDCU-8877, GDCU-8834, GDCU-8929, GDCU-8931. High Priority: GDCU-8842, GDCU-8900, GDCU-8861, GDCU-8865, GDCU-8849. Genesis release goes first.

meeting_summaries.title: Mid Week Catchup — 6th May 2026
meeting_summaries.time: TBC (Microsoft 365 auth not available; Confluence page updated ~11:00 BST based on context)
meeting_summaries.summary: Weekly cross-functional product and dev sync. Mike Pett attended alongside Chloe Christie, Ciara Carroll, Gabriel Cornoiu, Josh Smith, Eoin McCarthy, Shane McGrath, and Shalini Kumar. Headline: Gabriel announced a new partnership agreement with Standard.co.uk (Indy group), requiring product/dev/design involvement for setup in the next sprint. Chloe confirmed tomorrow's 14-ticket release list (Genesis first, then GDC). Two CWV tickets in the current cycle (GDCU-8874, GDCU-8925) were confirmed acceptable. Alberta regulation ticket created (GDCU-8946). Interlinking requests raised for Country Link Grid (Strategy and Slot Reviews) and Local Navs for News Pages. A dev issue query was raised on GDCU-8933. Kelvin's focus for Thursday–Friday confirmed as Dean/Colin prototype concepts, so GDCU-8878 (CA/FR oplist) will not complete before Thursday. Note: Michael Butlin was not in this meeting. Mike addressed GDCU-8619 with Michael Butlin separately via group DM at 16:04.
decisions: Standard.co.uk (Indy group) partnership agreed — Eoin McCarthy to create product/dev/design setup tickets for next sprint; offers UI adjustment and AdTech dashboard setup required
Bloomreach push notifications ticket to be moved to next sprint by Chloe — Kelvin is focused Thursday and Friday on Dean/Colin prototype concepts
Kelvin will not finish CA/FR oplist (GDCU-8878) before Thursday — resuming next week; confirmed OK
Two CWV tickets (GDCU-8874 and GDCU-8925) in the current cycle — confirmed acceptable
14 tickets confirmed for tomorrow's release — Genesis platform deploys first
Links by Country shortcode brainstorm agreed between Gabriel and Eoin (Michael Butlin to provide anchor text variety spreadsheet)
action_items_from_meeting: Eoin McCarthy: create Standard.co.uk setup tickets for next sprint
Chloe Christie: move Bloomreach push notifications ticket to next sprint
Eoin McCarthy: brainstorm solution for Links by Country shortcode (currently hardcoded, same anchor texts on all GX slot review pages)
Mike / Product: plan involvement for Standard.co.uk integration — design, matrix, and AdTech dashboard needed

meeting_summaries.title: Agenda May 6th 2026 — Product Sync (Michael Butlin meeting)
meeting_summaries.summary: Separate product sync attended by Ciara Carroll, Corey Cunningham, Michael Butlin, Diana Thurmann, Gabriel Cornoiu, Dan Smullen, Dean Ryan, and Joshua Smith. Mike Pett was not listed on this meeting's attendee roster — the morning report had flagged this as an unblock opportunity with Michael Butlin, but that conversation happened separately via group DM at 16:04 instead. The agenda page (last updated ~09:09 BST) contained only Chloe Christie's World Cup item. No post-meeting notes were published to Confluence.
decisions: No recorded decisions — no meeting notes published post-meeting
action_items_from_meeting: Mike addressed GDCU-8619 with Michael Butlin directly via DM at 16:04 — Ciara confirmed a solution is in progress

action_points.action: CONFIRM TOMORROW'S RELEASE — 14 tickets going live Thu 07/05 per Chloe (Mid Week Catchup + #6amigos). BAU: GDCU-8895, GDCU-7363, GDCU-8611, GDCU-8854, GDCU-8912, GDCU-8877, GDCU-8834, GDCU-8929, GDCU-8931. High Priority: GDCU-8842, GDCU-8900, GDCU-8861, GDCU-8865, GDCU-8849. Genesis platform deploys first — monitor #6amigos and #gdc-product-design-pod tomorrow morning for any release issues. GDCU-8834 Cool Tones is a partial release; remaining comment-section fixes deferred to next week.
REVIEW GDCU-8806 In-App Age Gate (18+ Verification) — Carlos Blauth, In Product since 15:18 today. New item in Mike's review queue. Medium priority; review and approve or provide feedback tomorrow.
REVIEW GDCU-8947 Cosmo Cash Machine Images — Ciara Carroll, In Product since 14:48 today. New review item. Ciara is already carrying GDCU-8619 (In Progress) — do not let GDCU-8947 age in the queue.

MONITOR GDCU-8619 OPERATOR LIST REDESIGN — Ciara said at 16:06: 'i have the solution, we discussed it Mike. just needs some updates so i'll sort that now.' Moved to In Progress. Mike's standing ask (05/05): 'post this iteration I want the next step to be to get this onto a QA environment.' Confirm Ciara completes updates and transitions to QA. If not In QA by Thursday EOD, escalate in Friday's 121 with Colin.

SCHEDULE WC KO CALL — Group Bets design (Ciara) was targeting today Thu 08/05. Confirm Group Bets design status with Ciara and schedule the kickoff call with devs — Mike agreed this with Chloe on 05/05. WC Predictor design (Miguel) targets Tue 12/05. Hard deadline: 11 June. Do not let the week end without a KO call date locked.

PREPARE FRIDAY 121 WITH COLIN (08/05) — Draft three specific asks: (1) GDCU-8619: Ciara is In Progress with a solution — do you still need to step in with Michael Butlin, or is this resolved? (2) Vic Dadson signed off until 16 May — who covers data engineering (Nemanja reqs, BI knowledge-sharing with Chris)? (3) WC risk: what happens if Group Bets or Predictor miss design targets? Frame the 10:19 frustration message ('blockers either get projects descoped to rubbish or not done at all') as specific asks, not a complaint.

STANDARD.CO.UK PARTNERSHIP — Review Eoin's setup tickets when created (next sprint). Gabriel's announcement confirmed product, design, and AdTech dashboard involvement. Ensure the intake form is used and scope is defined before sprint planning. Mike should align with Gabriel on timeline and any Indy group-specific offer UI requirements.

CHECK MIGUEL IS BACK & WC PREDICTOR DESIGN STARTED — No Slack activity from Miguel today (second consecutive day of no visibility). WC Predictor design must start this week; target Tue 12/05. Confirm first thing Thursday that Miguel is active and has the brief — if not, the 12/05 target is at risk.

KARL CHECK-IN — WC matrix data (fixture dating & resulting) is a dependency for both Group Bets and WC Predictor. Mike told Chloe on 05/05 he has a check-in with Karl 'this week to confirm' data is exposed. Still outstanding — do this Thursday or the week is missed.

MONITOR GDCU-8907 Betting Tips Hub (Sanja Budic) — In QA at 17:00 today but marked Blocked priority. Hot-flagged. Investigate what is blocking and whether Mike's input is needed. Does this affect the sprint close or the WC timeline?

MONITOR GDCU-8878 Oplist Redesign CA/FR (Kelvin Yee, In QA at 16:32) — Mid Week Catchup confirmed Kelvin will not complete this before Thursday (focused on Dean/Colin prototypes). Resumes next week. Confirm sprint plan updated and no stakeholder expectation mismatch.

INTAKE FORM — Gabriel Cornoiu's 23/04 suggestions (SEO & Information Architecture for new pages/hubs; operator offer attributes) still unconfirmed. Gabriel attended today's Mid Week Catchup — good context now that the Standard.co.uk item is active. Confirm intake form is updated this week.

GDCU-8956 Logo Generator for Claude DS — New ticket created by Chloe at 16:59, assigned to Ciara Carroll (To Do). Monitor: Ciara's plate currently includes GDCU-8619 (In Progress), GDCU-8947 (In Product), GDCU-8846 (To Do), and WC Group Bets design. A prioritisation conversation with Ciara may be needed.

CREATIVE DIRECTION — GDCU-8846 Cosmo Spins Spring Ad Campaign (Ciara Carroll, To Do). Josh Kerr feedback: needs rethinking — clearer message, less 'gaming' feel. Still unstarted. With GDCU-8956 now added to Ciara's queue, provide creative direction this week before her capacity is fully consumed.

This week it is the most important 121 in weeks: Mike sent Colin a frustration message at 10:19 today ('blockers at the moment are pretty frustrating'), Vic Dadson is signed off until 16 May, GDCU-8619 has a partial resolution but is not in QA yet, and WC has a hard 11 June deadline with outstanding data and KO call.

(3) Confirm Karl data check completed. (4) Have WC risk timeline ready (Group Bets design → KO call → dev → June 11). Be specific about what Colin needs to decide or unlock — this is not a catch-up, it's an escalation meeting.
